title: UI & HUD Fragments
description: Top-level UI shell, HUD fragments, chat, minimap, menus, and serialization for game stats display

key_relationships: UI composes all dialog instances (AboutDialog, PausedDialog, etc.)
StatValues creates StatValue (factory pattern – all static methods return StatValue lambdas)
StatValue is interface with single method display(Table)
StatValues depends_on UnlockableContent, Item, Liquid, Block, BulletType, Weapon, Ability
key_patterns: UI.init() creates all fragment instances and calls fragment.build(group) to wire them into the scene graph
menuGroup (visible in menu state) hosts MenuFragment; hudGroup (visible in game state) hosts HudFragment, ChatFragment, MinimapFragment, PlayerListFragment, ConsoleFragment
UI.loadAnd() shows LoadingFragment then runs task after 7f ticks
HudFragment.showUnlock() animates an unlock notification table
HudFragment.showToast() schedules a toast overlay via scheduleToast()
StatValues is a pure utility class – no instance state; each static method returns a StatValue lambda that receives a Table and populates it
StatValue lambdas are used by the stats/content-info system to display block stats in the UI (ContentInfoDialog)
important_notes: UI is the central shell: it owns all fragment and dialog references. Fragments follow a build(Group) pattern – no shared interface/base class. ChatFragment and ConsoleFragment extend Table directly. StatValues factory methods compose Table widgets using arc.scene.ui layout API. HudFragment references Vars.ui.hudfrag.shown for announcement visibility guard.
